summary: The plan selects the next coherent Symphony/Codexter follow-up: align serial Ralph with BoardAdapter and ComputeSelector without implementing parallel dispatch or an external scheduler.

user_story_groups.story: As the operator, I want Ralph to keep draining one safe local ticket at a time without accidentally ignoring compute constraints.
user_story_groups.evidence: The plan preserves serial phase handoff and adds compute blockers before selection.

user_story_groups.story: As a future integrator, I need Ralph and invocation to share the same WorkItem and compute vocabulary.
user_story_groups.evidence: The plan routes active candidate loading through FileTicketAdapter and compute admission through select_compute().

user_story_groups.story: As a reviewer, I need proof that old selector behavior still works while future targets block clearly.
user_story_groups.evidence: The plan requires fixture tests for current local_shared selection, archive dependency satisfaction, local_worktree setup hints, and unsupported future targets.

rubric_sections.name: implementation-plan
rubric_sections.next_action: Build the whole ticket, not a smaller selector-only slice.

rubric_sections.name: evidence-quality
rubric_sections.next_action: Attach selector JSON and test output during implementation.

next_action: Wait for user approval, then run $impl on TASK-0119.
